tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive exited 0 with --gov-mode production --deployment organizational --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh from project dir reports Context7 MCP configured; 54 OK / 0 fix / 0 manual

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh --non-interactive from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' message (PR #18 holds)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh --non-interactive --gov-mode production sets poc_mode="production" in phase-state.json, which blocks Phase 4 release
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh --non-interactive --project foo --platform desktop --deployment organizational --gov-mode production --language rust --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --project-dir /tmp/foo --allow-existing-dir; jq .poc_mode /tmp/foo/.claude/phase-state.json (returns "production"); bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 (rejects with 'Phase 4 is blocked - project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3.')
framework_bugs.expected: Production deployment with --gov-mode production should yield phase-state.json with poc_mode: null (matching the interactive flow at init.sh:381 which sets POC_MODE="" for the Production governance choice). Phase 4 release should be unblocked.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh:3217 in non-interactive mode does POC_MODE="$GOV_MODE" unconditionally, so --gov-mode production becomes POC_MODE=production, then phase-state.json poc_mode="production", then start_phase4 (process-checklist.sh:540) refuses because any non-null poc_mode is treated as a POC. The fix is to mirror the interactive translation at init.sh:381: POC_MODE="" when GOV_MODE=production; only sponsored_poc/private_poc become POC_MODE values.

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially with no artifact requirements enforced for phase1

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: current_phase remained at 0. No script auto-advances .current_phase after gate passes (U-D bug from prior sweep is still open and unfixed).

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after Phase 1 gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all phase1_architecture steps and bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/check-phase-gate.sh exits 0, jq .current_phase .claude/phase-state.json still returns 0
framework_bugs.expected: Some script should advance .current_phase from 0 to 2 (or 1) when the gate passes and approval is logged
framework_bugs.actual: Phase remains at 0 forever; check-phase-gate.sh is a consistency checker only, not an advancer. This is the U-D regression from 2026-04-25; expected to remain open per runbook.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 build_loop steps completed sequentially; security_audit artifact (docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md) accepted

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: Direct git commit after 5 steps and tests/source stubs succeeded

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m "feat(bl006-trigger): no loop"` succeeded with no Build Loop. Expected per runbook: U-C still open. Note: pre-commit-gate.sh as a hook also exited 0 here because process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message only enforces feat:-prefix when current_phase >= 2 (and current_phase is 0 due to U-D).

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + --complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, .build_loop.feature reset to null and a new --start-feature 'uat-feat-2' succeeded (PR #17 BL-006 grace window holds)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With proper sentinel schema (question/options/recommendation/offered_at), pre-commit-gate.sh returns permissionDecision=deny with 'pending user decision'. Note: a sentinel that only had approval_type/created_at/context/blocking_action fields was treated as 'malformed' but still blocked — useful conservative behavior but the schema mismatch in the runbook example deserves a doc note.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent gate invocation returned exit 0 (allow)

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 (PreToolUse hook is the only enforcement)
framework_bugs.reproduction: git commit --allow-empty -m "feat(bl006-trigger): no loop" — succeeds without invoking pre-commit-gate.sh because the shell doesn't route through Claude Code's PreToolUse path. There is no commit-msg/pre-commit git hook installed.
framework_bugs.expected: feat: commits without a recorded Build Loop should be refused at the git layer too, e.g. via a .git/hooks/commit-msg installed by init.sh that calls process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message.
framework_bugs.actual: Bypass is trivial; this is the U-C regression from prior sweep, expected to remain open. Note: at current_phase=0 the policy is no-op anyway; even if a git-level hook existed it would only start enforcing once phase advances.

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-015 sentinel JSON schema is documented only by reading pending-approval.sh source. The required keys are question/options/recommendation/offered_at — not the approval_type/created_at/context/blocking_action fields one might guess from the field names. A schema doc would help.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/pending-approval.sh:138-148 (writes the schema); no companion doc found via grep for pending-approval.json schema.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: Warns about needing UAT after recorded feature but exits 0

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh passed on a stub HTML containing one scenario after meeting state-restatement opening + 60+ char expected; first attempt with simple stub failed cleanly with two violations

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits 0; all 9 phase3_validation steps completed (with stub semgrep/integration/accessibility/performance result files)

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: BLOCKED on first attempt by 'Phase 4 is blocked - project is in production mode' because phase-state.json poc_mode was set to "production" by init.sh non-interactive (see Critical bug in phase 0). After manually patching phase-state.json poc_mode to null, --start-phase4 exited 0.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: After unblocking, all 6 PHASE4_STEPS (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) completed sequentially with stub artifacts (HANDOFF.md with monitoring section, RELEASE_NOTES.md, docs/test-results/*_rollback-test.md, docs/test-results/*_handoff-test.md)

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: phase4_release:handoff_written artifact check did require HANDOFF.md; same step blocked when HANDOFF.md absent (verified by code path at process-checklist.sh:333-339)

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: .current_phase still 0 (U-D regression — no script advances phase). All 6 phase4_release steps completed but phase-state.json doesn't reflect 'released'.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Only matches in committed files were CI variable references (e.g. ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}) and TODO comments in release.yml — no concrete credential values. Live-secret regex (after stripping framework/template/doc paths) returned 0 hits.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A: no FORCE/reset events occurred; .claude/process-audit.log absent as expected

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD == d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (unchanged from start). Untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ in $FRAMEWORK predate this run (parent-shell preexisting state per startup git status snapshot); no new tracked-file modifications.

summary: Critical regression in PR #19 BL-016: --gov-mode production maps to poc_mode="production" in phase-state.json, blocking Phase 4 release for the very deployment scenario it should permit; otherwise full base/production/desktop/full track flow runs cleanly with U-C and U-D still open as expected.
